action.reasoning: Observation: baseline eval is strong at 0.7907 balanced accuracy after only 3 epochs, with especially weaker few-shot accuracy (0.6674) relative to many/medium. Analysis: the current recipe already learns well, so the best next gain is likely from longer training plus stronger long-tail handling rather than a risky architecture swap. Weighted sampling + logit adjustment may be over-correcting head classes while still not maximizing tail performance; adding mixup/cutmix would complicate loss handling, so a safer improvement is deferred re-weighting (DRW) with class-balanced loss in later epochs and a longer cosine schedule. Given ~80s for 3 epochs, 10-12 epochs should fit comfortably and provide a meaningful improvement signal. Decision: extend train.py with optional class-balanced loss and DRW, keep convnext baseline, train 12 epochs, locally smoke-test predict, and if training succeeds submit the new best checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 12 --batch_size 64 --lr 3e-4 --loss_name drw_cb_ce --drw_start_epoch 7 --label_smoothing 0.05 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output pred_smoke.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('pred_smoke.json')); print(len(d['predictions']), d['predictions'][:5])"
